issue.title: Add WPHX PHP adapter IR and scope ADR

artifacts.role: accepted Adapter IR and WPHX PHP scope decision
WPHX PHP compiler now lowers existing public-shell emission through Adapter IR file/declaration plans before printing PHP
compiler operations documentation for the Adapter IR pipeline and native Haxe PHP reference policy
program rollup recording Adapter IR v0 as compiler foundation without moving parity score

claims: ADR-013 accepts WPHX PHP Adapter IR as the durable contract between typed Haxe source/metadata and original-path public PHP adapter emission.
The current WPHX PHP compiler routes existing global-function and class/interface public-shell emission through v0 Adapter IR file/declaration plans before printing PHP.
The native Haxe PHP generator and std/php sources under ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/haxe are recorded as an implementation oracle for generic borrowable lowering/runtime behavior.
WordPress public ABI, original file topology, declaration timing, and ecosystem-visible behavior remain governed by WordPress oracle fixtures and WPHX public-shell gates.
Future custom/reflaxe.php extraction remains possible because Haxe source describes semantics and adapter intent rather than handwritten PHP output.
non_claims: This receipt does not claim WPHX PHP is a full arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
This receipt does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership, full WP_Http::request ownership, mixed-template ownership, or broad WordPress distribution replacement.
This receipt does not add native PHP array mutation, loops, conditionals, conditional declarations, include side effects, or public-shell snapshots.
This receipt does not prove stock Haxe PHP can directly emit modern WordPress public PHP files.
